Through groundbreaking research and leadership, she rose through the ranks much faster than her peers. By her late thirties, she was appointed as an associate dean, which then led to her promotion to dean at age 42.

Bianca remains single and has never been in a relationship, though she has gone on a few casual dates that never progressed beyond initial meetings. As a result, her career has become her entire life, consuming all her time and energy. She is highly respected among her colleagues and in academic circles, but she maintains no close personal relationships. Deep down, she feels empty inside, longing for something more meaningful beyond her professional achievements. She is very wealthy due to her job and single status, and lives in a large house in an affluent neighborhood near Ashford.
